Question:
We plan to conduct a logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy using NHANES data from the years 2011 to 2020. 
The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin or diabetic retinopathy status.
The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles(%) Q1: <25, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75, Q4: ≥75. 
The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including age at screening.
Structured Question:
Analysis Type: logistic regression

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2020

Inclusion Criteria:
- age in years at screening >= 30

Exclusion Criteria:
- age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
- uncertain diabetes status, defined as:
    "Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?" = "borderline" AND (glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) is missing OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%)
- missing serum albumin (g/dL)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status

Covariates:
- age at screening

Predictor:
- serum albumin (g/dL) categorized into quartiles:
  Q1: <25% Q2: ≥25 and <50% Q3: ≥50 and <75% Q4: ≥75%

Outcome:
- diabetic retinopathy = 1 if "Yes"; 0 if "No"

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age at screening", "age in years at screening"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.p_demo
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening ON temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age when first told the patient had diabetes"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.p_diq
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes ON temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.p_diq
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes ON temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "glycohemoglobin (hba1c) (%)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_glycohemoglobin;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_glycohemoglobin AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.p_ghb
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_glycohemoglobin ON temp_master_glycohemoglobin (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "serum albumin (g/dL)", "serum albumin (g/dL)", "serum albumin (g/dL)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_albumin_g_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.p_biopro
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_albumin_g_dl ON temp_master_albumin_g_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "diabetic retinopathy", "diabetic retinopathy", "diabetic retinopathy status"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.p_diq
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.diq_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.diq_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.diq_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy ON temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy (respondent_sequence_number);

-- ALL UNIQUE PATIENT IDs
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_all_ids;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_all_ids AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_glycohemoglobin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_albumin_g_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy;

-- Add index to the main ID table for the final join
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_all_ids_respondent_sequence_number ON temp_all_ids (respondent_sequence_number);

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t1."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes",
    t2."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t3."glycohemoglobin",
    t4."albumin_g_dl",
    t5."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_albumin_g_dl t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);
-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants if their age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NULL OR f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
-- Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?”
-- and if they answer as "borderline" and either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (
    f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5)
);

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude anyone with missing data on diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Covariate: age at screening
-- Extract age at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Predictor: serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT
  s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.3 Outcome: diabetic retinopathy
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No"
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
         WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
         WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
         ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  tas."age_at_screening",
  tsa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  tdr."diabetic_retinopathy"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_age_at_screening tas ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tas."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles tsa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tsa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy tdr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = tdr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: View Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

R:
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("diabetic_retinopathy")
    
    cohort <- df


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["diabetic_retinopathy"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(cohort), function(col_name) {
        summarize_variable(cohort[[col_name]], col_name)
    })
    )

    identifier_vars  <- report$variable[report$category == "identifier"]
    cat("Identifier Variables:\n");   print(identifier_vars);   cat("\n")

    

    binary_vars      <- report$variable[report$category == "binary"]
    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")
    print(numerical_vars)

    
    

    outcome_var     <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("outcome")]
    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")
    

    
        
    
    
    # Identify predictors and factor candidates
    predictor_vars <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary", "numerical")]
    factor_candidates <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]

    # Convert binary/categorical variables to factor if not already
    for (var in factor_candidates) {
    if (!is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        df[[var]] <- as.factor(df[[var]])
    }
    }

    # Initialize reference_levels table
    reference_levels <- tibble(variable = character(), reference_level = character())

    # Clean, trim, and set reference levels for all factor variables
    for (col in factor_candidates) {
    df[[col]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[col]])))
    levels_vec <- levels(df[[col]])

    # Try to find preferred reference
    preferred_order <- c("healthy", "none", "control", "reference", "ref", "normal","Q1", "low", "0", "1")
    matches <- tolower(levels_vec)  # lower case to standardize
    ref_level <- NA  # default

    for (keyword in preferred_order) {
    idx <- which(grepl(keyword, matches, ignore.case = TRUE))
    if (length(idx) > 0) {
        ref_level <- levels_vec[idx[1]]  # pick the first match for that keyword
        break  # stop at the highest-priority match
    }
    }


    # If none found, fall back to most common level
    if (is.na(ref_level)) {
        level_counts <- table(df[[col]])
        ref_level <- names(level_counts)[which.max(level_counts)]
    }

    # Set reference level
    df[[col]] <- relevel(df[[col]], ref = ref_level)

    # Record reference level
    reference_levels <- bind_rows(
        reference_levels,
        tibble(variable = col, reference_level = ref_level)
    )
    }
    
        
    
    x_raw <- df[[outcome_var]]
    x_chr <- trimws(tolower(as.character(x_raw)))

    none_tokens <- c("none", "nan", "na", "", "null", "nil", "missing")
    x_chr[x_chr %in% none_tokens] <- NA_character_

    y <- rep(NA_integer_, length(x_chr))

    y[x_chr %in% c("yes", "y", "true", "t")]  <- 1L
    y[x_chr %in% c("no",  "n", "false", "f")] <- 0L

    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_chr))
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 1] <- 1L
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 0] <- 0L

    idx <- is.na(y) & !is.na(x_chr)
    if (any(idx)) {
    vals <- sort(unique(x_chr[idx]))

    if (length(vals) != 2) {
        stop(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' must have exactly 2 non-missing classes after excluding none. Found: %s",
        outcome_var, paste(vals, collapse = ", ")
        ))
    }

    tab <- table(x_chr[idx])
    minority <- names(tab)[which.min(tab)]
    majority <- setdiff(vals, minority)

    cat(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' recoding: 1 = %s (minority)  0 = %s (majority)",
        outcome_var, minority, majority
    ))

    y[idx] <- as.integer(x_chr[idx] == minority)
    }

    df[[outcome_var]] <- y

    # Must contain both 0 and 1 somewhere (plus NA allowed)
    u <- sort(unique(df[[outcome_var]][!is.na(df[[outcome_var]])]))
    if (!(length(u) == 2 && all(u == c(0, 1)))) {
    stop(sprintf(
        "After recoding, '%s' must contain both 0 and 1 (plus NA for none). Found: %s",
        outcome_var,
        ifelse(length(u) == 0, "<no non-missing values>", paste(u, collapse = ", "))
    ))
    }

    formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", paste(predictor_vars, collapse = " + ")))
                
    
        
    
    model <- glm(formula,
                data = df,
                family =  binomial(link = "logit"))
            
    
    result_table <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
        select(term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)
Variables: [{'variable': 'respondent_sequence_number', 'category': 'identifier', 'n_unique': 2964, 'na_count': 0, 'summary': 'Total rows = 2964'}, {'variable': 'age_at_screening', 'category': 'numerical', 'n_unique': 51, 'na_count': 0, 'summary': 'mean = 63.626, sd = 12.343'}, {'variable': 'serum_albumin_g_dl', 'category': 'categorical', 'n_unique': 4, 'na_count': 0, 'summary': 'Q1: <25%: 23.8%; Q2: ≥25 and <50%: 20.6%; Q3: ≥50 and <75%: 24.1%; Q4: ≥75%: 31.6%'}, {'variable': 'diabetic_retinopathy', 'category': 'outcome', 'n_unique': 2, 'na_count': 0, 'summary': '0: 79.7%; 1: 20.3%'}]
